original_prompt: Read the content on webpage www.informations.com.
assessment_reasoning: The instruction is short but clear: it specifies a single target webpage to read. For an agent that can fetch webpages this provides almost all needed information (the exact page to request). It omits minor details like URL scheme (http/https) and what to do with the content after reading (summarize, extract, or return raw), so there is slight ambiguity about expected output format or follow-up action. However, for the ground-truth verification (simply requesting the URL) the prompt is sufficiently specific.

variations.L1: Take a look at that website I mentioned and tell me what's there.
variations.L2: Look up www.informations.com and tell me what it contains.
variations.L3: Read the content on the webpage www.informations.com and give me a short summary of the main points.
variations.L4: Open https://www.informations.com, read the full page content, and provide a concise summary (3–5 sentences) plus any important links you find on the page.
variations.L5: Use the get_webpage function to fetch https://www.informations.com (get_webpage(url='https://www.informations.com')). Extract the page title, all headings (h1–h6), and the main body text, then return a JSON object with fields: {"title": string, "headings": [string], "body": string}. Do not request or fetch other URLs.
